faqs.q: How long does a typical MacBook repair take?
faqs.a: Most common repairs like batteries and SSDs are completed same-day. Logic board work may take 2-3 business days depending on parts.

faqs.q: Do you repair Macs still under Apple warranty?
faqs.a: We repair all Macs, but if yours is under warranty we recommend Apple first. Out-of-warranty and vintage Macs are our specialty.

faqs.q: Can you recover data from a Mac that won't boot?
faqs.a: Yes. We extract data from dead logic boards and failing drives. If the storage chip is intact, your files are almost always recoverable.

faqs.q: What does a diagnostic cost?
faqs.a: Diagnostics are a flat fee, credited toward the repair if you proceed. No hidden charges and no obligation.

the-ponds.ctaText: Get a no-obligation diagnostic and know exactly what's wrong before you pay.

the-ponds.localInfo: The Ponds' young demographic means high Apple device adoption. We frequently service MacBook Airs for students and MacBook Pros for working parents in this vibrant community.

faqs.q: Do you handle Macs damaged by power surges?
faqs.a: Regularly. Power surges damage voltage regulators and charging circuits. We diagnose the specific failed components and replace them.

faqs.q: My Mac is practically new — should I go to Apple instead?
faqs.a: If it's under warranty, try Apple first. For out-of-warranty or accidental damage not covered by AppleCare, we're often faster and more affordable.

faqs.q: Can you help set up a new Mac for a home office?
faqs.a: Yes. We handle data migration, peripheral setup, and optimisation to ensure your new Mac runs efficiently from day one.

faqs.q: How do I protect my Mac from construction dust?
faqs.a: We recommend regular compressed air cleaning and keeping the Mac elevated. If dust has already entered, we do thorough internal cleaning.

faqs.q: Do you repair Macs on weekends?
faqs.a: Contact us to arrange weekend drop-off or collection. We accommodate busy schedules wherever possible.

faqs.q: Do you offer student pricing?
faqs.a: We keep all repairs affordable. Our component-level approach means lower costs than shops that replace entire assemblies unnecessarily.

faqs.q: Can you fix a Mac that got wet?
faqs.a: Yes. Water damage requires immediate board-level cleaning to prevent corrosion spread. The sooner you bring it in, the better the outcome.

faqs.q: How do I get to you by public transport?
faqs.a: We're accessible from the station and major bus routes. Contact us for specific directions from your stop — it's straightforward.

faqs.q: What if the repair costs more than the Mac is worth?
faqs.a: We'll tell you honestly. If a repair doesn't make financial sense, we'll say so and help you recover your data instead.

faqs.q: Can you fix multiple family Macs at once?
faqs.a: Yes. Bring them all in together. We can diagnose each one and give you a combined quote so you know the full picture upfront.

faqs.q: My kid's MacBook screen cracked — is it expensive?
faqs.a: Screen costs vary by model but we always quote before starting. MacBook Air screens are generally more affordable than Pro Retina displays.

faqs.q: Is it worth putting an SSD in my old iMac?
faqs.a: Almost always. An SSD transforms a slow iMac into a responsive machine. It's the single best upgrade for most older Macs.

faqs.q: Do you wipe data during repairs?
faqs.a: Not unless necessary. Most repairs don't touch your data at all. If a repair requires it, we'll warn you first and offer backup options.
